user_goals_summary: This user values academic writing where every structural unit earns its place through argumentative necessity rather than procedural convention. They prioritize reader orientation (understanding why before what), closing force (paragraphs that cash out their claims), and writing that carries intellectual weight even in conventionally descriptive sections like methods.

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Orientation
rubric.description: The reader must understand what a paragraph is *for* before being asked to process its details. Opening sentences establish purpose and warrant, so the reader never feels handed information before knowing why it matters.

dimensions.label: Opening sentence(s) establish why the paragraph exists before presenting specifics
The reader can identify what the paragraph is arguing or establishing within the first two sentences
The paragraph does not require re-reading to understand its purpose
New information is introduced with enough context that its relevance is immediately apparent

rubric.name: Closing Force
rubric.description: Paragraphs end by cashing out their warrant — stating what the preceding content means, enables, or makes possible to claim. The reader should never finish a paragraph thinking 'okay, but so what?'

dimensions.label: The paragraph's closing sentence states what the content means or enables, not just what it describes
The final sentence does not merely restate details or dissolve into abstraction without resolution
The final paragraph of a section connects to the broader argument or signals what the design makes possible
Closers emphasize consequence or implication rather than summarizing what was already said

rubric.name: Argumentative Necessity
rubric.description: Every paragraph carries argumentative weight, advancing a claim rather than listing procedural steps. Methodological and structural choices are presented as theoretically motivated — chosen for reasons, not by convention.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph advances an argument or claim, not just reports a procedure
Design and methodological choices are presented as motivated by the research question or theoretical commitments
Content is organized around reasoning rather than sequenced as procedural steps
Each paragraph would leave a visible gap in the argument if removed

rubric.name: Section-Level Arc
rubric.description: The section as a whole reads as building toward a destination, not accumulating information. Paragraph order follows argumentative logic, and later paragraphs deepen or extend what earlier ones established.

dimensions.label: The section reads as building toward a destination rather than accumulating details
The sequence of paragraphs follows a clear argumentative or conceptual progression
Later paragraphs draw on, deepen, or complicate what earlier paragraphs established
The section's ending feels like an arrival — something has been argued, not just described

rubric.name: Precision and Terminological Consistency
rubric.description: Technical terms are used consistently without unexplained variation. Claims are carefully scoped and specific. Details are accurate and unambiguous where specificity is needed.

dimensions.label: Key terms are used consistently throughout without unexplained synonymic variation
Claims are specific and carefully scoped, avoiding vague or overreaching language
Technical details and procedural descriptions are accurate and unambiguous
Where specificity is required, the writing provides it rather than gesturing vaguely

rubric.name: Intellectual Voice
rubric.description: The register is formal but not bureaucratic. The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading, and authorial reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate, not default.

dimensions.label: Writing maintains academic formality without becoming bureaucratic or wooden
Claims are stated with confidence appropriate to their evidence, not hedged into meaninglessness
The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading
The authors' reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate and motivated, not conventional

coaching_notes: This user diagnoses structural problems with striking precision — they articulate not just that something feels off but *why* (e.g., 'landing in the middle of a point'). Trust their paragraph-level flow instincts; that is where their feedback is most actionable and most strongly felt. Non-negotiable: the reader must understand what a paragraph is *for* before processing its details. They will push for argumentative weight in sections others treat as merely descriptive, and they respond best when the writing earns intellectual ambition rather than defaulting to safe procedural reporting.

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Orientation
rubric.description: The reader must understand what a paragraph is *for* — and how it follows from what came before — before being asked to process its details. Opening sentences establish both purpose and logical dependency: they tell the reader why this paragraph exists *at this point in the argument*, not merely what it is about. A well-oriented paragraph cannot be reordered without breaking the logic.

New information is introduced with enough context that its relevance is immediately apparent

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Orientation
rubric.description: The reader must understand what a paragraph is *for* — and how it follows from what came before — before being asked to process its details. rubric.name: Methods Section Narrative Rhythm
rubric.description: Methods sections follow a four-beat structure within each substantive paragraph or paragraph cluster: (1) the motivating question or problem that makes this methodological choice necessary, (2) an overview of the approach taken, (3) the formal or technical details, and (4) a brief closing statement of what this approach enables or buys the analysis. Skipping the motivation makes the section feel like a manual; skipping the payoff leaves the reader wondering why the detail mattered. This rhythm is non-negotiable for methods writing.

dimensions.label: Each methods paragraph or cluster opens by establishing the motivating question or problem
An approach overview precedes formal technical details
Formal details are present and accurate but follow, not lead, the motivating framing
A closing 'what this buys us' sentence makes explicit what the methodological choice enables
The section reads as a sequence of argued decisions, not a procedural checklist

rubric.name: Argumentative Necessity
rubric.description: Every paragraph carries argumentative weight, advancing a claim rather than listing procedural steps. Methodological and structural choices are presented as theoretically motivated — chosen for reasons, not by convention.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph advances an argument or claim, not just reports a procedure
Design and methodological choices are presented as motivated by the research question or theoretical commitments
Content is organized around reasoning rather than sequenced as procedural steps
Each paragraph would leave a visible gap in the argument if removed

rubric.name: Closing Force
rubric.description: Paragraphs end by cashing out their warrant — stating what the preceding content means, enables, or makes possible to claim. The reader should never finish a paragraph thinking 'okay, but so what?'

dimensions.label: The paragraph's closing sentence states what the content means or enables, not just what it describes
The final sentence does not merely restate details or dissolve into abstraction without resolution
The final paragraph of a section connects to the broader argument or signals what the design makes possible
Closers emphasize consequence or implication rather than summarizing what was already said

rubric.name: Strategic Register Modulation
rubric.description: Formal academic register is the baseline and must be maintained throughout. However, a rare plain-language aside — at most one or two per section — can function as a signal of deep understanding, demonstrating that the author can explain a complex point simply. This is not an invitation to be casual; it is a precision tool. Overuse collapses the formal register entirely and is treated as a defect, not a feature.

dimensions.label: Formal register is consistently maintained as the baseline throughout the section
Plain-language asides, if present, are limited to one or two per section and placed at moments of genuine conceptual complexity
No casual language appears outside of a deliberate, isolated plain-language moment
Any informal moment reads as confident authorial choice, not as an accidental lapse in register

rubric.name: Precision and Terminological Consistency
rubric.description: Technical terms are used consistently without unexplained variation. Claims are carefully scoped and specific — where the writing is arguing toward a demonstrated conclusion, stronger verbs are preferred; where results motivate future directions rather than prove them, appropriately scoped language is required. Precision means accurate scope, not automatically stronger claims: epistemic honesty, including appropriate hedging when pointing toward future work rather than proven conclusions, is itself a form of precision. Details are accurate and unambiguous where specificity is needed. Filler hedges — vague qualifications inserted because the author was unsure where information belonged — are treated as a precision failure, not a stylistic choice.

dimensions.label: Key terms are used consistently throughout without unexplained synonymic variation

Claims are specific and carefully scoped, avoiding vague or overreaching language — where the writing argues toward a demonstrated conclusion, stronger verbs are preferred; where results motivate future directions rather than prove them, appropriately scoped language is required
Technical details and procedural descriptions are accurate and unambiguous
Where specificity is required, the writing provides it rather than gesturing vaguely (this does not apply to hedges that accurately reflect the strength of a claim)
No filler hedges are present — qualifications appear only where they carry genuine epistemic meaning, not as placeholders for unresolved organizational decisions

Claims about established findings use confident language; claims about future directions or recommendations use language that accurately reflects their epistemic status (e.g., 'motivate' rather than 'establish' when results point toward but do not prove a conclusion)

rubric.name: Section-Level Arc
rubric.description: The section as a whole reads as building toward a destination, not accumulating information. Paragraph order follows argumentative logic, and later paragraphs deepen or extend what earlier ones established.

dimensions.label: The section reads as building toward a destination rather than accumulating details
The sequence of paragraphs follows a clear argumentative or conceptual progression
Later paragraphs draw on, deepen, or complicate what earlier paragraphs established
The section's ending feels like an arrival — something has been argued, not just described

rubric.name: Intellectual Voice
rubric.description: The register is formal but not bureaucratic. The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading, and authorial reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate, not default. Confidence is calibrated to the argument's actual warrant: demonstrated conclusions are stated with conviction, while forward-looking claims, recommendations, or results that point toward but do not prove a conclusion are expressed with language that accurately reflects their epistemic status.

dimensions.label: Writing maintains academic formality without becoming bureaucratic or wooden
Claims are stated with confidence appropriate to their evidence, not hedged into meaninglessness
The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading
The authors' reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate and motivated, not conventional

Confidence is calibrated to the argument's actual warrant — demonstrated conclusions are stated with conviction, while forward-looking claims and recommendations are expressed with language that accurately reflects their epistemic status rather than overclaiming

user_goals_summary: This user values academic writing where every structural unit earns its place through argumentative necessity rather than procedural convention. They prioritize reader orientation (understanding why before what), closing force (paragraphs that cash out their claims), and writing that carries intellectual weight even in conventionally descriptive sections like methods.
coaching_notes: This user diagnoses structural problems with striking precision — they articulate not just that something feels off but *why* (e.g., 'landing in the middle of a point'). Trust their paragraph-level flow instincts; that is where their feedback is most actionable and most strongly felt. Non-negotiable: the reader must understand what a paragraph is *for* before processing its details. They will push for argumentative weight in sections others treat as merely descriptive, and they respond best when the writing earns intellectual ambition rather than defaulting to safe procedural reporting.

user_goals_summary: The user writes academic paper sections (methods, discussion, etc.) that function as arguments rather than procedural summaries. They prioritize reader orientation — the reader must understand why each paragraph exists and why it appears at this point before processing its content. Closings must deliver a crisp payoff, and epistemic claims must be scoped with precision that reflects their actual evidential warrant.

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Orientation
rubric.description: The reader must understand what a paragraph is for — and how it follows from what came before — before being asked to process its details. Opening sentences establish both purpose and logical dependency, telling the reader why this paragraph exists at this point in the argument. A well-oriented paragraph cannot be reordered without breaking the logic.

dimensions.label: Opening sentence(s) establish why the paragraph exists before presenting specifics
The reader can identify what the paragraph is arguing or establishing within the first two sentences
The opening establishes a consequential connection to the preceding paragraph — not just a topic transition, but a dependency that makes the paragraph feel necessary at this position
Conceptual framing or approach overview precedes formal technical specifics within the paragraph
The paragraph does not require re-reading to understand its purpose

rubric.name: Closing Force
rubric.description: Paragraphs end by cashing out their warrant — stating what the preceding content means, enables, or makes possible to claim. The reader should never finish a paragraph thinking 'okay, but so what?' When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight rather than bundled into a single trailing clause, and the most significant or novel point is positioned last for maximum impact.

dimensions.label: The paragraph's closing sentence states what the content means or enables, not just what it describes
The final sentence does not merely restate details or dissolve into abstraction without resolution
When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight — they are not compressed into a single trailing clause that dilutes both
The most significant or novel point is positioned last to close with maximum impact
The final paragraph of a section connects to the broader argument or signals what the analysis makes possible

rubric.name: Argumentative Necessity
rubric.description: Every paragraph carries argumentative weight, advancing a claim rather than listing procedural steps or summarizing results. Methodological, structural, and interpretive choices are presented as theoretically motivated — chosen for reasons, not by convention. The section reads as a sequence of argued decisions, not a procedural checklist or a summary of findings.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph advances an argument or claim, not just reports a procedure or finding
Design and interpretive choices are presented as motivated by the research question or theoretical commitments
Content is organized around reasoning rather than sequenced as procedural steps or result summaries
Each paragraph would leave a visible gap in the argument if removed

rubric.name: Epistemic Precision
rubric.description: Claims are scoped to match their actual evidential warrant. Demonstrated conclusions are stated with conviction; empirical observations pointing toward future directions use language that reflects recommendation, not proof. Precision means accurate scope — not automatically stronger claims. Filler hedges inserted from organizational uncertainty are treated as a precision failure, while hedges that honestly reflect the strength of a claim are required.

dimensions.label: Claims about demonstrated findings use confident, direct language without unnecessary hedging
Claims about future directions or recommendations use language reflecting their epistemic status (e.g., 'motivate' rather than 'establish' when results point toward but do not prove a conclusion)
Key terms are used consistently throughout without unexplained synonymic variation
No filler hedges are present — qualifications appear only where they carry genuine epistemic meaning, not as placeholders for unresolved decisions
Technical details and procedural descriptions are accurate and unambiguous where specificity is needed

This is a precision tool, not an invitation to be casual. Overuse collapses the formal register entirely and is treated as a defect.

dimensions.label: Formal register is consistently maintained as the baseline throughout the section

No casual language appears outside of a deliberate, isolated plain-language moment
Any informal moment reads as confident authorial choice that crystallizes a complex point, not as an accidental lapse in register

Paragraph order follows argumentative logic such that reordering would collapse the reasoning. Later paragraphs deepen or extend what earlier ones established, and the section's ending feels like an arrival.

dimensions.label: The section reads as building toward a destination rather than accumulating details
The paragraph sequence creates logical dependencies such that reordering would break the argument
Later paragraphs draw on, deepen, or complicate what earlier paragraphs established
The section's ending feels like an arrival — something has been argued, not just described

rubric.name: Intellectual Voice
rubric.description: The register is formal but not bureaucratic. The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading, and authorial reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate, not default. The voice conveys an author who understands the material deeply enough to make structural and interpretive choices with visible confidence.

dimensions.label: Writing maintains academic formality without becoming bureaucratic or wooden
The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading
The author's reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate and motivated, not conventional
The writing conveys an author who commands the material and makes structural choices with earned confidence

coaching_notes: This user's most consistent complaint is being given content before understanding why it matters — orientation is non-negotiable and should be checked first in every paragraph. They treat epistemic honesty as a form of precision, not weakness: overclaiming empirical findings as proven conclusions is as serious an error as under-claiming demonstrated results. They respond well to being offered structural options with clear reasoning for each, and they make decisive choices about ordering and emphasis — expect them to have strong opinions about what closes a paragraph or section.

rubric.name: Methods Section Narrative Rhythm
rubric.description: Methods sections must follow a specific four-beat rhythm within each substantive paragraph or passage: (1) motivating question — why this choice matters to the research; (2) approach overview — a plain statement of what was done; (3) formal details — the technical specifics; (4) payoff — a brief statement of what this approach enables or buys. Skipping the motivation collapses the passage into a manual; skipping the payoff leaves the reader without a reason to have read the details. This rhythm applies at the paragraph level and should be discernible across the section as a whole.

dimensions.label: Each substantive methods paragraph opens with a motivating question or research-driven reason for the choice being described
An overview of the approach is stated plainly before formal technical details are introduced
Formal technical details follow the overview and are presented with sufficient precision to be unambiguous
Each methods paragraph closes with a 'what this buys us' sentence — a brief statement of what the choice enables analytically or empirically
The section as a whole reads as a sequence of argued decisions, not a procedural checklist

Each paragraph would leave a visible gap in the argument if removed

rubric.name: Section-Level Arc
rubric.description: The section as a whole reads as building toward a destination, not accumulating information. Paragraph order follows argumentative logic such that reordering would collapse the reasoning. Later paragraphs deepen or extend what earlier ones established, and the section's ending feels like an arrival.

dimensions.label: The section reads as building toward a destination rather than accumulating details
The paragraph sequence creates logical dependencies such that reordering would break the argument
Later paragraphs draw on, deepen, or complicate what earlier paragraphs established
The section's ending feels like an arrival — something has been argued, not just described

rubric.name: Epistemic Precision
rubric.description: Claims are scoped to match their actual evidential warrant. Demonstrated conclusions are stated with conviction; empirical observations pointing toward future directions use language that reflects recommendation, not proof. Precision means accurate scope — not automatically stronger claims. Filler hedges inserted from organizational uncertainty are treated as a precision failure, while hedges that honestly reflect the strength of a claim are required. Inconsistent terminology is a precision failure of equal severity to miscalibrated claim strength.

dimensions.label: Claims about demonstrated findings use confident, direct language without unnecessary hedging
Claims about future directions or recommendations use language reflecting their epistemic status (e.g., 'motivate' rather than 'establish' when results point toward but do not prove a conclusion)

Key terms are used consistently throughout — synonymic variation for stylistic reasons is treated as a terminology error, not a virtue. Note: consistent terminology does not license replacing a key term with a pronoun or shorthand when the referent distance within the sentence is large enough to require backtracking — in those cases, repetition of the term is the precise choice.
No filler hedges are present — qualifications appear only where they carry genuine epistemic meaning, not as placeholders for unresolved organizational decisions
Technical details and procedural descriptions are accurate and unambiguous where specificity is needed

rubric.name: Closing Force
rubric.description: Paragraphs end by cashing out their warrant — stating what the preceding content means, enables, or makes possible to claim. The reader should never finish a paragraph thinking 'okay, but so what?' When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight rather than bundled into a single trailing clause, and the most significant or novel point is positioned last for maximum impact.

dimensions.label: The paragraph's closing sentence states what the content means or enables, not just what it describes
The final sentence does not merely restate details or dissolve into abstraction without resolution
When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight — they are not compressed into a single trailing clause that dilutes both
The most significant or novel point is positioned last to close with maximum impact
The final paragraph of a section connects to the broader argument or signals what the analysis makes possible

rubric.name: Strategic Register Modulation
rubric.description: Formal academic register is the baseline and must be maintained throughout. However, a rare plain-language aside — strictly one or two per section — can function as a signal of deep understanding, demonstrating that the author can explain a complex point simply. This is a precision tool deployed at moments of genuine conceptual difficulty, not an invitation to be casual. Overuse collapses the formal register entirely and is treated as a defect equal in severity to pervasive casualness.

dimensions.label: Formal register is consistently maintained as the baseline throughout the section
Plain-language asides, if present, are strictly limited to one or two per section and placed at moments of genuine conceptual complexity — not scattered for approachability
No casual language appears outside of a deliberate, isolated plain-language moment
Any informal moment reads as confident authorial choice that crystallizes a complex point, not as an accidental lapse in register
Overuse of plain-language asides (more than two per section) is flagged as a register failure regardless of whether individual instances are well-placed

rubric.name: Intellectual Voice
rubric.description: The register is formal but not bureaucratic. The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading, and authorial reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate, not default. The voice conveys an author who understands the material deeply enough to make structural and interpretive choices with visible confidence.

dimensions.label: Writing maintains academic formality without becoming bureaucratic or wooden
The writing engages with ideas at a level that rewards careful reading
The author's reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate and motivated, not conventional
The writing conveys an author who commands the material and makes structural choices with earned confidence

Sentences are syntactically clean — no run-on constructions that bundle multiple distinct claims or qualifications into a single clause. However, when a key technical term has just been named and a pronoun substitute would create referent ambiguity in a precision-sensitive context, repeating the term is preferred over syntactic compression — clarity of reference takes precedence over tightness.

user_goals_summary: The user writes academic paper sections (methods, discussion, etc.) that function as arguments rather than procedural summaries. They prioritize reader orientation — the reader must understand why each paragraph exists and why it appears at this point before processing its content. Closings must deliver a crisp payoff, and epistemic claims must be scoped with precision that reflects their actual evidential warrant.
coaching_notes: This user's most consistent complaint is being given content before understanding why it matters — orientation is non-negotiable and should be checked first in every paragraph. They treat epistemic honesty as a form of precision, not weakness: overclaiming empirical findings as proven conclusions is as serious an error as under-claiming demonstrated results. They respond well to being offered structural options with clear reasoning for each, and they make decisive choices about ordering and emphasis — expect them to have strong opinions about what closes a paragraph or section.

writing_type: academic research papers (computational linguistics / NLP venues)
user_goals_summary: The user writes formal academic papers for top-tier NLP venues and values writing where every paragraph carries argumentative weight, readers are oriented before receiving detail, and logical structure is tight enough that paragraphs cannot be reordered without breaking the reasoning. They prize epistemic precision — claims scoped precisely to their evidential warrant — and a confident authorial voice that occasionally deploys strategic plainness to crystallize complex points.

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Orientation
rubric.description: The reader must understand what a paragraph is for — and how it follows from what came before — before being asked to process its details. Opening sentences establish both purpose and logical dependency, so the reader knows why this paragraph exists at this point in the argument. A well-oriented paragraph cannot be reordered without breaking the logic.

The reader can identify what the paragraph is arguing or establishing within the first two sentences
The opening establishes a consequential connection to the preceding paragraph — not just a topic transition, but a dependency that makes the paragraph feel necessary at this position
Conceptual framing or approach overview precedes formal technical specifics within the paragraph
The paragraph does not require re-reading to understand its purpose

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Argumentative Rhythm
rubric.description: Each substantive paragraph follows a discernible four-beat rhythm: (1) a motivating question or problem that establishes why this content matters to the argument; (2) a plain overview of the approach, claim, or position; (3) the formal or technical specifics; (4) a closing statement of what this enables, establishes, or changes. Skipping the motivation collapses the paragraph into a manual; skipping the payoff leaves the reader without a reason to have read the details. This rhythm applies across section types — methods, discussion, and related work alike.

An overview of the approach or claim is stated plainly before formal technical details are introduced

rubric.description: Paragraphs end by cashing out their warrant — stating what the preceding content means, enables, or makes possible to claim. The reader should never finish a paragraph thinking 'okay, but so what?' When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight rather than bundled into a single trailing clause, and the most significant or novel point is positioned last for maximum impact.

dimensions.label: The paragraph's closing sentence states what the content means or enables, not just what it describes
The final sentence does not merely restate details or dissolve into abstraction without resolution
When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight — they are not compressed into a single trailing clause that dilutes both
The most significant or novel point is positioned last to close with maximum impact
The final paragraph of a section connects to the broader argument or signals what the analysis makes possible

rubric.name: Authorial Voice and Register
rubric.description: Formal academic register is the baseline and must be maintained throughout. The voice conveys an author who commands the material — choices are presented as deliberate, not default, and reasoning is visible. A rare plain-language aside (strictly one or two per section) functions as a precision tool at moments of genuine conceptual difficulty, crystallizing a complex point simply and signaling that the formalism is a choice, not a crutch. Overuse of plain language collapses the register and is treated as a defect equal in severity to pervasive casualness. Strategic informality is a scalpel, not a default mode.

dimensions.label: Formal academic register is consistently maintained as the default throughout — no accidental casual language

Plain-language asides, if present, are strictly limited to one or two per section and placed at moments of genuine conceptual complexity — each earns its informality by crystallizing a difficult point that formal phrasing obscures
Plain-language asides, when used, do not appear in consecutive paragraphs — spacing them is required to preserve their impact and the section's formal register
The author's reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate and motivated, not conventional or default
The writing conveys command of the material and makes structural and interpretive choices with earned confidence
Sentences are syntactically clean — no run-on constructions that bundle multiple distinct claims into a single clause; clarity of reference takes precedence over syntactic compression

coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: paragraph openings must orient the reader before delivering content — the user will reject otherwise strong prose that buries its warrant. This user distinguishes sharply between filler hedges (unacceptable) and epistemic calibration (required); logical connectors like 'therefore' that mark inferential dependency must never be treated as filler, and claim-strength words like 'motivate' vs. 'establish' carry real meaning. The user responds best when offered multiple options with reasoning for each, makes decisive choices when given clear alternatives, and appreciates when structural inconsistencies are flagged openly rather than silently patched.

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Orientation
rubric.description: The reader must understand what a paragraph is for — and how it follows from what came before — before being asked to process its details. Opening sentences establish both purpose and logical dependency, so the reader knows why this paragraph exists at this point in the argument. A well-oriented paragraph cannot be reordered without breaking the logic.

dimensions.label: Opening sentence(s) establish why the paragraph exists before presenting specifics
The reader can identify what the paragraph is arguing or establishing within the first two sentences
The opening establishes a consequential connection to the preceding paragraph — not just a topic transition, but a dependency that makes the paragraph feel necessary at this position
Conceptual framing or approach overview precedes formal technical specifics within the paragraph
The paragraph does not require re-reading to understand its purpose

rubric.name: Epistemic Precision
rubric.description: Claims are scoped to match their actual evidential warrant. Demonstrated conclusions are stated with conviction; empirical observations pointing toward future directions use language that reflects recommendation, not proof. Filler hedges inserted from organizational uncertainty are a precision failure — the single most reliable signal that the author did not know where to place information. This does not apply to logical connectors that mark inferential dependency between a stated argument and its conclusion. Hedges that honestly reflect claim strength are required. Inconsistent terminology is a precision failure of equal severity to miscalibrated claim strength.

dimensions.label: Claims about demonstrated findings use confident, direct language without unnecessary hedging
Claims about future directions or recommendations use language reflecting their epistemic status (e.g., 'motivate' rather than 'establish' when results point toward but do not prove a conclusion)

Key terms are used consistently throughout — synonymic variation for stylistic reasons is treated as a terminology error; when referent distance is large enough to require backtracking, the full term is repeated rather than replaced with a pronoun
No filler hedges are present — qualifications appear only where they carry genuine epistemic meaning, not as placeholders for unresolved organizational decisions

Hedged phrasing does not appear where a clear claim is warranted — phrases like 'it could be argued' or 'one might suggest' are used only when the author genuinely cannot claim stronger warrant, not as defensive softening of well-supported points
Technical details and procedural descriptions are accurate and unambiguous where specificity is needed

rubric.name: Argumentative Necessity
rubric.description: Every paragraph carries argumentative weight, advancing a claim rather than listing procedural steps or summarizing prior work neutrally. Methodological, structural, and interpretive choices are presented as theoretically motivated — chosen for reasons, not by convention. Abstract claims are anchored with concrete specifics that prevent them from floating.

dimensions.label: Each paragraph advances an argument or claim, not just reports a procedure or finding
Design and interpretive choices are presented as motivated by the research question or theoretical commitments
Content is organized around reasoning rather than sequenced as procedural steps or neutral literature summaries
Each paragraph would leave a visible gap in the argument if removed
Abstract or theoretical claims are anchored with concrete specifics — examples, languages, mechanisms — that prevent them from floating

rubric.name: Paragraph-Level Argumentative Rhythm
rubric.description: Each substantive paragraph follows a discernible four-beat rhythm: (1) a motivating question or problem that establishes why this content matters to the argument; (2) a plain overview of the approach, claim, or position; (3) the formal or technical specifics; (4) a closing statement of what this enables, establishes, or changes. Skipping the motivation collapses the paragraph into a manual; skipping the payoff leaves the reader without a reason to have read the details. This rhythm applies across section types — methods, discussion, and related work alike — and is not optional in methods sections.

dimensions.label: Each substantive paragraph opens with a motivating question or argument-driven reason for the content that follows
An overview of the approach or claim is stated plainly before formal technical details are introduced
Technical details follow the overview and are presented with sufficient precision to be unambiguous
Each paragraph closes with a statement of what the content enables, establishes, or changes — not just a restatement of what was described
The section as a whole reads as a sequence of argued decisions, not a procedural checklist or literature summary

Logical connectors marking consequence ('therefore,' 'thus,' 'hence') are preserved when they reflect genuine inferential structure between a preceding argument and its conclusion — they are not filler and must not be removed

rubric.name: Section-Level Arc
rubric.description: The section as a whole reads as building toward a destination, not accumulating information. Paragraph order follows argumentative logic such that reordering would collapse the reasoning. Later paragraphs deepen or extend what earlier ones established, and the section's ending feels like an arrival — something has been argued, not just described.

dimensions.label: The section reads as building toward a destination rather than accumulating details
The paragraph sequence creates logical dependencies such that reordering would break the argument
Later paragraphs draw on, deepen, or complicate what earlier paragraphs established
The section's ending feels like an arrival — something has been argued, not just described

rubric.name: Authorial Voice and Register
rubric.description: Formal academic register is the baseline and must be maintained throughout. The voice conveys an author who commands the material — choices are presented as deliberate, not default, and reasoning is visible. A rare plain-language aside (strictly one or two per section) functions as a precision tool at moments of genuine conceptual difficulty, crystallizing a complex point simply and signaling that the formalism is a choice, not a crutch. Overuse of plain language collapses the register and is treated as a defect equal in severity to pervasive casualness. Strategic informality is a scalpel, not a default mode.

dimensions.label: Formal academic register is consistently maintained as the default throughout — no accidental casual language

Plain-language asides, if present, are strictly limited to one or two per section and placed at moments of genuine conceptual complexity — each earns its informality by crystallizing a difficult point that formal phrasing obscures
Plain-language asides, when used, do not appear in consecutive paragraphs — spacing them is required to preserve their impact and the section's formal register
The author's reasoning is visible — choices are presented as deliberate and motivated, not conventional or default
The writing conveys command of the material and makes structural and interpretive choices with earned confidence
Sentences are syntactically clean — no run-on constructions that bundle multiple distinct claims into a single clause; clarity of reference takes precedence over syntactic compression

rubric.name: Closing Force
rubric.description: Paragraphs end by cashing out their warrant — stating what the preceding content means, enables, or makes possible to claim. The reader should never finish a paragraph thinking 'okay, but so what?' When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight rather than bundled into a single trailing clause, and the most significant or novel point is positioned last for maximum impact.

dimensions.label: The paragraph's closing sentence states what the content means or enables, not just what it describes
The final sentence does not merely restate details or dissolve into abstraction without resolution
When a closing addresses multiple ideas, each is given its own weight — they are not compressed into a single trailing clause that dilutes both
The most significant or novel point is positioned last to close with maximum impact
The final paragraph of a section connects to the broader argument or signals what the analysis makes possible

user_goals_summary: The user writes argued academic prose where every paragraph earns its place through logical necessity rather than convention. They prioritize paragraph-level orientation (the reader must know why they're reading something before encountering the details), precise epistemic calibration (claim strength must match evidence), and strategic formal register with rare plain-language asides that signal command rather than casualness. Endings — both paragraph-level and section-level — must deliver payoff, not trail off.
coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: paragraph openings must orient the reader before delivering content — the user will reject otherwise strong prose that buries its warrant. This user distinguishes sharply between filler hedges (unacceptable) and epistemic calibration (required); logical connectors like 'therefore' that mark inferential dependency must never be treated as filler, and claim-strength words like 'motivate' vs. 'establish' carry real meaning. The user responds best when offered multiple options with reasoning for each, makes decisive choices when given clear alternatives, and appreciates when structural inconsistencies are flagged openly rather than silently patched.
